title: 10 best herbal teas for cracked heels
intro_desc: Herbal teas for cracked heels are infusions of natural herbs that are used to treat and prevent cracked heels. These teas are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to nourish and moisturize the skin, promoting healthy skin growth and reducing the appearance of cracked heels. Examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for cracked heels include chamomile, calendula, and lavender teas. These teas can be used as a foot soak or applied directly to the affected areas using a compress. By using herbal teas for cracked heels, individuals can experience improved skin health, reduced pain and discomfort, and an overall improvement in foot health and appearance. This can lead to increased confidence, mobility, and a better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with cracked heels because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that promote wound healing. The tea's active compounds, including flavonoids and carotenoids, stimulate tissue regeneration and cell growth. These properties make pot marigold tea an effective natural remedy for treating dry, cracked skin on the heels. The tea's soothing and moisturizing effects help to soften and exfoliate the dead skin cells, promoting healthy and smooth skin. Drinking pot marigold tea or using it as a foot soak can provide relief from cracked heels, leaving your feet feeling rejuvenated and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for cracked heels as inflammation can exacerbate dryness and cracking. The anti-inflammatory properties come from flavonoids and triterpenes present in the plant.

Antimicrobial: The tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection in cracked heels. Active constituents, such as flavonoids and essential oils, contribute to these antimicrobial effects.
Emollient: Pot marigold tea has an emollient effect, helping to soothe and soften dry, cracked skin. This is due to the presence of mucilage, a substance that forms a soothing film over the skin.

Wound-healing: The tea promotes wound healing, which is crucial for cracked heels. Active constituents like flavonoids, triterpenes, and carotenoids found in pot marigold contribute to its wound-healing properties.

Circulation-boosting: Pot marigold tea can help improve circulation, which is important for cracked heels as poor circulation can lead to dryness and cracking. The flavonoids and essential oils in the tea are thought to be responsible for its circulation-boosting effects.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, promoting skin cell regeneration and healing cracked heels.
Leaves: Contain high levels of essential oils, providing moisturizing and soothing effects, aiding in the treatment of dry and cracked heels.
Roots: Packed with nutrients and active compounds, stimulating skin repair and regeneration, addressing the symptoms of cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 1 tablespoon of dried pot marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the flowers, leaving only the tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak your cracked heels in the tea for 15-20 minutes, then pat dry.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with cracked heels because it contains allantoin, a substance that promotes new skin cell growth and helps to repair damaged tissues. The anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties of comfrey tea also soothe and hydrate the dry, cracked skin on your heels. Drinking comfrey tea or using it as a foot soak can provide relief and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for cracked heels. The vitamins and minerals found in comfrey tea, such as vitamin A, C, and E, also contribute to skin health and repair, making it an ideal choice for treating cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to its rosmarinic acid and tannins, helps alleviate pain and swelling associated with cracked heels.
Astringent: The astringent property of comfrey tea, caused by its tannins, tightens and protects damaged skin, promoting healing of cracked heels.
Demulcent: Comfrey tea's demulcent property, from mucilage content, soothes and moisturizes the skin, reducing dryness and cracking of heels.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of comfrey tea, owing to rosmarinic acid and allantoin, prevents infection in cracked heels and supports skin repair.
Cell proliferation: Comfrey tea's cell proliferation stimulation, attributed to allantoin, encourages skin cell growth and regeneration, accelerating healing of cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of allantoin, which promotes skin cell growth and helps heal cracked heels.
Rhyzomes: Rich in nutrients and rooted in the soil, they provide minerals that aid in skin regeneration.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, they help protect skin from damage and support healing.
Roots: Full of rosmarinic acid, they reduce inflammation and provide relief for dry, cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2-3 dried comfrey leaves into small pieces, releasing their healing properties.
Place the chopped leaves in a teapot, adding 1 cup of boiling water for each leaf.
Steep the comfrey leaves in hot water for 15-20 minutes, allowing the tea to become potent.
Strain the comfrey tea into a bowl, discarding the spent leaves.
Soak cracked heels in the warm comfrey tea for 15 minutes, promoting healing and softening the skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with cracked heels because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that soothe and heal damaged skin. The tea is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that promote skin cell regeneration and moisturize dry, cracked skin. Drinking plantain tea or using it as a foot soak can reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief from painful cracked heels. The herbal tea's emollient properties help soften and exfoliate dead skin cells, revealing smoother and softer skin. Regular use of plantain tea can improve the overall health and appearance of cracked heels, leaving them feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for cracked heels as inflammation can exacerbate skin dryness and cracking. The anti-inflammatory properties come from compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection in cracked heels. The antimicrobial properties are due to the presence of compounds like tannins, saponins, and flavonoids.

Astringent: Plantain tea acts as an astringent, helping to tighten and protect skin tissue. This property is important for cracked heels as it helps to promote skin cell regeneration and assists in healing. The astringent properties are primarily due to the tannins present in plantain.

Emollient: Plantain tea has emollient properties that help to soothe and soften skin. This is important for cracked heels as it helps to moisturize and protect the skin, promoting healing. The emollient properties are attributed to the mucilage content in plantain.

Antioxidant: Plantain tea contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage. This property is important for cracked heels as it promotes overall skin health and assists in the healing process. The antioxidant properties are primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, plantain leaves can help soothe and heal cracked heels.
Rhizomes: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, rhizomes can help reduce irritation and redness in cracked heels.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and allantoin, seeds can promote tissue regeneration and accelerate the healing of cracked heels.
Bark: With astringent properties, bark can help tighten and protect the skin, reducing the appearance of cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves from an herbal garden, rinse them thoroughly, and let them dry.
Tear the leaves into small pieces, place them in a pot, and add 4 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, reduce heat, and let the mixture simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a bowl, discarding the plantain leaves, and allow it to cool.
Soak both feet in the tea for 15 minutes, then pat heels dry and apply a moisturizer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tea helps with cracked heels because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can improve the health of your skin. The tea's nutrients, such as vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium, potassium, and magnesium, can promote skin cell regeneration and help to strengthen the skin on your feet. Drinking stinging nettle tea can also boost your overall skin health, which can help to prevent and heal cracked heels. By nourishing and protecting the skin on your feet, stinging nettle tea can provide relief and promote the healing of cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle tea reduces inflammation, which is important for cracked heels as it helps alleviate pain and swelling associated with cracked skin. The anti-inflammatory properties come from several active constituents, including flavonoids, polyphenols, and lectins.

Astringent: Nettle tea has astringent properties that help tighten and dry out excess moisture, promoting skin cell contraction and healing. Tannins in nettle leaves are responsible for its astringent effects.

Antimicrobial: Stinging nettle tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, which is crucial for cracked heels as it prevents infection in the fissures. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to various compounds, such as flavonoids, tannins, and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Nettle tea contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting skin repair and regeneration. The antioxidant properties are primarily due to the presence of polyphenols, flavonoids, and carotenoids.

Nutrient-rich: Stinging nettle tea is packed with vitamins and minerals essential for skin health, including vitamin A, C, and K, as well as calcium, magnesium, and iron. These nutrients support skin cell regeneration and collagen production, which are important for healing and preventing cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the stinging nettle tea plant are rich in vitamins and minerals, making them an ideal part of the plant to use in making medicinal tea for cracked heels.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the stinging nettle tea plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe and heal cracked heels.
Stems: The stems of the stinging nettle tea plant contain high levels of silica, which can help strengthen and repair the skin on cracked heels.
Flowers: The flowers of the stinging nettle tea plant are rich in antioxidants, which can help protect and heal damaged skin on cracked heels.
Seeds: The seeds of the stinging nettle tea plant are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help moisturize and nourish the skin on cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh stinging nettle leaves, avoiding contact with the plant's tiny hollow hairs that can cause skin irritation.
Rinse the nettle leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or insects.
Crush or chop the nettle leaves to release their healing properties.
Steep the crushed nettle leaves in 4 cups of boiling water for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid, discarding the leaves, and let it cool down before soaking your cracked heels for 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with cracked heels because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal dry, cracked skin. The tea is rich in flavonoids, tannins, and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation, fight infection, and promote tissue regeneration. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a foot soak can help increase moisture levels in the skin, improve elasticity, and prevent cracking. Additionally, yarrow tea may help improve circulation, which can further support healthy skin and tissue repair. Regular use of yarrow tea can help keep feet soft, smooth, and free from cracking.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent properties, due to its tannin content, help to tighten and dry out excessively cracked skin.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory effects of yarrow tea, attributed to its flavonoid content, reduce swelling and irritation in cracked heels.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties, resulting from its volatile oil content, prevent infection in cracked heels.
Wound healing: The wound healing properties of yarrow tea, owing to its lactone content, accelerate skin regeneration and tissue repair.
Circulation promotion: Yarrow tea's circulation promoting effects, attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content, enhance blood flow and nutrient delivery to cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the yarrow tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing and healing cracked heels.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers contain therapeutic compounds that promote skin cell regeneration, helping to repair damaged skin on cracked heels.
Stems: The stems of the yarrow tea plant contain essential oils and tannins that can help tighten and strengthen the skin on cracked heels, preventing further damage.
Rhyzomes: Yarrow rhyzomes have astringent properties that can help dry out excess moisture in cracked heels, promoting faster healing.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are packed with nutrients and antioxidants that can nourish and protect the skin on cracked heels, promoting overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried yarrow herb.
In a teapot, steep the yarrow in 2 cups of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a heat-safe bowl.
Soak a clean washcloth in the yarrow tea.
Apply the soaked washcloth to cracked heels for 10 minutes, twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog rose tea helps with cracked heels because it contains high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants that promote skin health. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and heal cracked heels, while its astringent properties help to tighten and tone the skin. Additionally, the tea's emollient properties can help to lock in moisture, keeping the skin soft and supple. Drinking dog rose tea can also promote overall skin health, leading to stronger and more resilient skin on the feet. The tea's natural healing properties make it a safe and effective treatment for cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dog rose tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of polyphenols and anthocyanins, helps reduce swelling and irritation in cracked heels.
Astringent: The astringent property of dog rose tea, caused by tannins, tightens and toughens the skin, promoting healing of cracked heels.
Emollient: Dog rose tea's emollient property, owing to its essential fatty acids content, helps moisturize and soothe dry, cracked skin.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of dog rose tea, from its vitamin C and bioflavonoids content, protects cracked heels from infection.
Antioxidant: Dog rose tea's antioxidant property, attributed to its high vitamin C and E content, aids in skin repair and regeneration, accelerating the healing of cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins and antioxidants, promoting skin cell regeneration and healing.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in soothing irritation and reducing redness.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, helping to moisturize and nourish dry, cracked skin.
Bark: Provides astringent properties, assisting in tightening and toning skin to improve cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 2 cups of fresh dog rose petals (discard the green parts) and 4 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove petals, then compost them.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak cracked heels in the tea for 15-20 minutes, then pat dry.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with cracked heels because it contains antiseptic, anti-fungal, and anti-inflammatory properties that promote wound healing and skin regeneration. The tea's soothing qualities also help alleviate dryness and itchiness, while its high levels of antioxidants and vitamins A, C, and E nourish and protect the skin from further damage. By regularly soaking your feet in lavender tea, you can soften the hard, dry skin on your heels, reduce inflammation, and promote the growth of healthy new skin. Moreover, the calming aroma of lavender has a stress-relieving effect that can help you relax and unwind, making your foot bath a rejuvenating and therapeutic experience.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic property: English lavender tea has antiseptic properties that help prevent cracked heels from getting infected, thanks to its active constituents like tannins, volatile oils, and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory property of English lavender tea helps reduce swelling and irritation in cracked heels, due to the presence of compounds like linalyl acetate and linalool.
Analgesic property: English lavender tea's analgesic property helps alleviate pain caused by cracked heels, as it contains active constituents like cineole and borneol.
Antifungal property: English lavender tea has antifungal properties that help control fungal infections in cracked heels, owing to its active constituents like camphor, geraniol, and limonene.

Emollient property: English lavender tea acts as an emollient, softening and moisturizing the skin, thereby promoting skin cell regeneration and healing of cracked heels, due to its active constituents like polyphenols and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, the leaves help soothe and heal cracked heels.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, the flowers aid in healing and protecting cracked heels.
Buds: High in nutrients and antioxidants, the buds promote skin cell regeneration and moisture retention in cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried English lavender flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it on cracked heels for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with cracked heels because it contains antiviral and antibacterial properties that can help to reduce inflammation and fight off any potential infections in the cracked skin. Additionally, lemon balm is rich in antioxidants that can help to promote the growth of new skin cells, which can aid in the healing of cracked heels. Furthermore, lemon balm has been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin, which can help to alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with cracked heels. Drinking lemon balm tea can also help to improve overall skin health, as it can increase hydration and promote the production of collagen, which can help to keep skin strong and supple.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Lemon balm tea's antimicrobial properties help prevent cracked heels by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause or worsen cracked heels. This property is important because it helps keep the skin free from infection and promotes healing. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and citronellal.

Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet, which can contribute to cracked heels. This property is important because it alleviates discomfort and allows for faster healing. The active constituents responsible for this property include eugenol and rosmarinic acid.

Antioxidant: Lemon balm tea contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting skin health and healing. This property is important because it helps maintain the integrity of the skin and supports the healing process. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Astringent: Lemon balm tea has astringent properties that help tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of cracked heels. This property is important because it helps improve the overall texture and appearance of the skin on the feet. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins.

Hydrating: Lemon balm tea has hydrating properties that help keep the skin moisturized and supple, preventing dryness and cracking. This property is important because it helps maintain the skin's natural barrier function and prevents the skin from becoming dry and brittle. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemon balm leaves help soothe and heal cracked heels.
Flowers: Containing high levels of antioxidants and essential oils, lemon balm flowers promote skin cell regeneration and moisture retention.
Stems: Packed with rosmarinic acid and other beneficial compounds, lemon balm stems aid in reducing inflammation and improving skin elasticity.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 2 cups of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried lemon balm leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down.
Soak a cloth in the cooled lemon balm tea and apply it on cracked heels for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with cracked heels because it has antiseptic and antifungal properties that can help to heal and prevent infections in dry, cracked skin. Thyme also contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help to soothe and protect the skin. Drinking thyme tea can help to hydrate the body and promote healthy skin from the inside out. Additionally, using thyme tea as a foot soak can help to soften and exfoliate dead skin, making it easier to remove and relieve cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Thyme tea has antiseptic properties that help prevent cracked heels from becoming infected, thanks to the thymol and other volatile oils present in thyme.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in cracked heels, as it contains flavonoids like apigenin, luteolin, and naringenin.
Antifungal: Thyme tea's antifungal properties help combat fungal infections that can exacerbate cracked heels, owing to the thymol and carvacrol content.
Astringent: Thyme tea's astringent properties help tighten and dry out excess moisture, preventing cracked heels, due to the tannins present in thyme.
Analgesic: Thyme tea's analgesic properties help alleviate pain from cracked heels, as it contains compounds like camphene, geraniol, and borneol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antiseptic and antifungal properties, making them effective in treating cracked heels caused by fungal infections.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to soothe and heal cracked heels.
Stems: Thyme stems have astringent properties that help to tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of cracked heels.
Rhyzomes: Thyme rhyzomes are packed with essential oils and nutrients that promote skin cell regeneration, aiding in the healing of cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried thyme herb to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the thyme tea into a cup, discarding the herb.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before soaking your cracked heels for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with cracked heels because it contains antiseptic and astringent properties that can help dry, cracked skin. The tannins found in sage leaves can help to tighten and strengthen the skin, reducing the appearance of cracked heels. Additionally, sage tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated skin. By drinking sage tea or using it as a foot soak, the nutrients and antioxidants in sage can be absorbed into the skin, promoting healing and hydration. Overall, sage tea can help to nourish and protect the skin on your feet, reducing the appearance of cracked heels and promoting healthy, soft skin.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Sage tea has antiseptic properties that help prevent cracked heels from getting infected, thanks to the presence of volatile oils like thujone, camphor, and eucalyptol.

Astringent: The astringent properties of sage tea help to tighten and strengthen the skin, reducing the likelihood of cracked heels by promoting skin cell cohesion, due to tannins present in the plant.
Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with cracked heels, as it contains rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in sage tea helps protect skin cells from damage and promotes skin cell regeneration, aiding in the healing of cracked heels, thanks to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and rosmarinic acid.

Circulation-boosting: Sage tea promotes blood circulation, which aids in delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the skin, facilitating skin cell repair and healing, due to the presence of essential oils and various flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and vitamins that promote skin cell regeneration and help heal cracked heels.
Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that improve blood circulation and aid in tissue repair, making them effective in treating cracked heels.
Bark: Contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to tighten and protect skin, reducing the appearance of cracked heels.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants and essential oils that soothe and moisturize the skin, providing relief for dry and cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 4 cups of water and 1/4 cup of dried sage leaves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 20 minutes.
After simmering, strain the liquid to remove the sage leaves.
Allow the sage tea to cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak your cracked heels in the sage tea for 15-20 minutes, then pat dry.

supplementary_best_treatment: 4. After soaking, gently scrub your heels with a pumice stone or foot file to remove dead skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cracked heels that are treated with herbal teas include dry, scaly, or rough skin on the feet. Certain herbal teas have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and heal cracked heels. For example, chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and irritation, while calendula tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection. To use, soak a cloth in cooled herbal tea and apply to the affected area for 10-15 minutes. Additionally, drinking herbal teas such as nettle or dandelion tea can help improve overall skin health by promoting hydration and providing essential vitamins and minerals. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies as they can interact with other medications and medical conditions.
